item.title: Chicago blues
item.description: Blues → Electric blues → Chicago blues Period: Late 1940s–present (peak: 1948–mid-1960s) Location: Chicago, Illinois (South Side and West Side) Key influences: Delta blues, country blues, jump blues RYM Muddy Waters’s slide guitar cutting through the noise of a South Side club at two in the morning,...

item.title: Folk rock
item.description: Rock → Folk rock Period: 1965–early 1970s; continuing currents since Location: Los Angeles, New York (Greenwich Village), London Key influences: American folk revival, Rock & roll, British Invasion pop, Bob Dylan RYM A twelve-string Rickenbacker picks out a chiming arpeggio over a steady backbea...

item.title: Rockabilly
item.description: Rock & roll → Rockabilly Period: 1954–1959; revival 1979– Location: Memphis, West Texas, Nashville, Norfolk, southern California Key influences: Country (honky-tonk, bluegrass, western swing), rhythm & blues, jump blues, boogie woogie, gospel RYM A guitar riff cuts through a short tape-delay...

item.title: Motown Records
item.description: Founded: 1959 Location: Detroit, Michigan (2648 West Grand Boulevard — “Hitsville U.S.A.”) Founder: Berry Gordy Jr.

item.title: Delta blues
item.description: Blues → Country blues → Delta blues Period: c. 1920s–1940s (recorded era); roots c.

item.title: Cultural capital
item.description: Two children walk into a museum on a school trip.

item.title: Cultural omnivorousness
item.description: A philosophy professor who keeps a Dolly Parton record next to the Bach on the shelf would have puzzled the sociologist of 1970.

item.title: Bourdieu — Distinction
item.description: Author: Pierre Bourdieu Title: Distinction: A Social Critique of the Judgement of Taste Year: 1984 (French original, La Distinction, 1979) Publisher: Harvard University Press Type: Book Bourdieu’s 1960s French survey asked people which of three pieces of music they most admired — Bach’s The Well-Tem...

item.title: Lott — Love and Theft
item.description: Author: Eric Lott Title: Love and Theft: Blackface Minstrelsy and the American Working Class Year: 1993 Type: Academic book (Oxford University Press) A study of blackface minstrelsy that reframes white America’s relationship with Black culture as simultaneously one of desire and domination.
